channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Judge awards $6.6 million to whistleblowers who reported Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton to FBI
item.description: A district court judge has ruled in favor of four whistleblowers who said that Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton retaliated against them for reporting bribery allegations, awarding his employees more than $6 million combined in damages.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump urges Americans to 'hang tough' on tariffs plan as markets tumble
item.description: President Donald Trump has called on Americans to "hang tough" amid stock market turmoil over the last few days and as China retaliates with 34% tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The frenzied 24 hours when Venezuelan migrants in the US were shipped to an El Salvador prison
item.description: It was just a few sentences in a meandering, hourlong presidential speech on a Friday afternoon. Along with talk about falling egg prices and a vow to expel “corrupt forces” from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: TIM SULLIVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's upcoming White House meeting with Netanyahu is expected to focus on Gaza and tariffs
item.description: PALM BEACH GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump plans to meet with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday in what would be their second White House sit-down since Trump's return to office.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin argues judge can't order return of man deported to El Salvador
item.description: A federal judge didn't have the authority to order the Trump administration to broker the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported from the U.S. to a notorious El Salvador prison, government attorneys argued Saturday as they urged an appeals court to suspend the ruling.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Ex-lawmaker George Santos faces 7-year prison sentence for federal fraud, identity theft
item.description: Disgraced ex-congressman George Santos could receive 7 years in prison after pleading guilty to federal fraud charges. Prosecutors allege he stole from donors and misused campaign funds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In the race to save lives after the Myanmar quake, US rescuers are notable by their absence
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Day after day, Chinese rescue teams haul children and elderly people from collapsed buildings as cameras beam the thanks of grateful survivors around the world. Russian medical teams show off field hospitals erected in a flash to tend the wounded.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Protesters tee off against Trump and Musk in 'Hands Off!' rallies across the US
item.description: Thousands of demonstrators are rallying at sites across the U.S. against President Donald Trump and billionaire Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sec Hegseth to visit Panama after Trump's demands for canal's return
item.description: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth will visit Panama next week for meetings with the country's leaders, following remarks from President Donald Trump about potentially acquiring the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump abruptly fires the 4-star general who headed the National Security Agency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has abruptly fired the director of the National Security Agency, according to U.S. officials and members of Congress, but the White House and the Pentagon have provided no reasons for the move.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hundreds of law firms, former judges sign onto briefs in support of Perkins Coie
item.description: The brief asks U.S. District Judge Beryl Howell to decide the case in the law firm’s favor and indefinitely block Trump’s order.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Divided Supreme Court sides with Trump to block teacher grants
item.description: A divided Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration by allowing officials to block $65 million in teacher development grants frozen over concerns they were promoting diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: About 500 law firms sign brief challenging Trump's executive orders targeting the legal community
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s executive orders targeting the legal community pose “a grave threat to our system of constitutional governance and to the rule of law itself,” according to a court filing submitted Friday by more than 500 law firms.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to give TikTok another 75-day extension
item.description: President Trump said Friday that he plans to give TikTok another 75-day extension as his administration nears a deal to avert a ban on the popular social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: States sue to block Trump's election order, saying it violates the Constitution
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democratic officials in 19 states filed a lawsuit against President Donald Trump's attempt to reshape elections across the U.S., calling it an unconstitutional invasion of states' clear authority to run their own elections.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: States sue to block Trump’s election order, saying it violates the Constitution
item.description: Democratic officials in 19 states are suing over President Donald Trump’s attempt to reshape elections across the U.S., calling it an unconstitutional invasion of states’ clear authority to run their own elections. Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison is one of the 19 officials suing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Here's Gov. Joe Lombardo's plan to solve the Nevada housing crisis
item.description: A $250 million plan to spur construction of homes and apartments for low- to middle-income Nevadans is projected to provide homes for 16,000 people, according to officials who presented it Wednesday night in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Despite backlash, Florida county term limit bill advances in the House
item.description: Florida voters may have the chance to set term limits, not just for state lawmakers, but also for county commissioners and school board members.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here's how newly imposed tariffs will affect California products
item.description: President Donald Trump’s widespread tariffs could have a profound impact on California’s exports, according to a study from the Public Policy Institute of California.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Senate confirms Oz as head of CMS along party lines
item.description: The Senate has confirmed Mehmet Oz as administrator for the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS), making him the head of Medicaid at a time where its funding is at risk of being severely cut. The Senate voted 53-45 along party lines to confirm Oz.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: After federal firings, officials worry about program that helps many Minnesotans pay for heat
item.description: Minnesota was scheduled to receive an additional $12 million to $13 million in LIHEAP funds to help another 10,000 households. If that payment is delayed, the program could run out of money by mid-April.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: State program connects those found incompetent to stand trial with mental health services
item.description: The program, which has now been in effect for a year, says it has found success in getting criminal suspects connected with mental health services.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Other DFL statewide officeholders break with Walz on return-to-office requirement
item.description: The attorney general, secretary of state and state auditor said they would keep existing telework rules in place because they are independent offices.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Fired or frustrated federal workers may have eager employers waiting for them in Minnesota
item.description: For federal workers, the past few months have been filled with uncertainty as the Trump administration pushes ahead with efforts to reduce the size of government agencies. But whether they are forced or choose to leave, those workers may find employers eager to hire them.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Deadlocked St. Paul City Council sends interim council member decision to mayor
item.description: There were four finalists who interviewed for the seat. Three council members are in favor of one candidate, while the remaining three support another candidate.
item.dc:creator: Regina Medina, MPR News

item.title: 5 takeaways from Tuesday’s elections, including bad news for Elon Musk
item.description: Democrats can take yesterday’s elections as one of the first bits of good news they’ve had in months. But there are lots of issues that are still center-right, including immigration.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News
